sections.title: Background
sections.text:
CompanyA is a retail organization that sells home decor products online. It also has stores at various locations throughout the world, where many employees help customers resolve their queries, return defective products, and initiate refunds. Currently, most of the processes and activities that are performed by employees are performed manually. Employees also struggle to get a unified view of a customer's information, as they have to open multiple applications and systems in order to complete an order placement.

CompanyA is planning to upgrade its outdated applications by adopting the Microsoft Dynamics 365 Sales app. It also wants to leverage the Power Platform low code capabilities. Its focus is to automate most of the sales process and reduce the effort required to handle customer queries and requests so that its employees can focus more on productive activities.

sections.title: Current Environment
sections.text:
CompanyA uses an Oracle database to store customer information, customer queries, complaints, and return/refund requests. All the communication related to order processing, confirmation, and delivery is currently handled manually by email.

The approval of product refund requests is also handled through email, so the refund process takes at least a month to be completed. Customer information has been distributed among different systems and these systems are not integrated with each other. Consequently, employees have to open all the applications to complete the process. Employees can only access the applications through their desktop computers and they add the information to Microsoft Excel spreadsheets while they navigate to the customer location.

sections.title: Requirements
sections.text:
General: CompanyA wants to design a configurable application that requires low-code/no-code development. The application must be connected with all the existing applications to get a 360-degree view of customer information such as profile, purchases, orders, queries, complaints, feedback, and refund requests.

Employees: Employees must be able to access the application through their mobile devices so that they can capture the information while at the customer location. Employees must be able to see the reports and analytics of sales data to track sales performance. Employees must be able to raise the requests on behalf of the customers from the Customer self-service portal and they must be able to chat with the customer to identify their needs. Employees who are working in the store must have a guided flow or action items to be followed to complete the sales process.

Communication: Once an order is placed, a confirmation email should be sent automatically to the customer, along with the order ID. For the customer to track the order, an email and SMS notification should be sent automatically when the order is processed and shipped.

Approval: Refund requests need to be auto-assigned to the approval team so that they can be processed. The approver must be able to approve or reject a request either through Microsoft Teams, Mobile App, or via email and they must have the option of providing a comment.

Users: Salespeople must be able to log in to their mobile app to view their daily appointments, customer visits, customer details, and also perform tasks. Salespeople must be able to track the customer's location while going for a meeting at the customer's address. The sales manager must be able to log in to their mobile app to approve/reject the salespeople's requests. The sales manager must be able to log in to their mobile app to track sales performance. A user with Global Admin rights must be able to manage users, assign roles, and create/remove users through the mobile app.

Customizations: In the mobile app, salespeople must not be able to see the features provided for the sales manager and vice versa. The application must generate a unique Order ID once an order is placed successfully. Customers' documents must be stored in an appropriate location or system so as not to consume Microsoft Dataverse storage. Customers' sensitive data available on the forms must be protected and should not be available to other employees. Customer data must be centralized in a common repository so that the employees can have a 360-degree view of the customer. The system should allow the importing of external data stored in Microsoft Excel spreadsheets. The system should also be accessible.

questions.q: You need to ensure that employees can access the Customer self-service portal to raise requests on behalf of customers. What authentication type should you use?
questions.why: The people accessing the portal are CompanyA EMPLOYEES (internal users with organizational accounts), so authenticate them with Microsoft Entra ID (the organization's identity provider). Microsoft Entra ID B2B is for inviting external partners/guests from other organizations. Microsoft Entra ID B2C is for external consumers/customers who self-register. Local authentication uses portal-managed local accounts, not appropriate for internal employees who already have Entra identities.

questions.q: CompanyA wants to have a common repository for customer data to provide a 360-degree view. You need to retrieve and transform the existing data from CompanyA's applications to Microsoft Dataverse. Which of the following component pairs should you use?
options: Custom Connector and Microsoft Graph API
Power Automate and Microsoft Graph API
Web Resource and Dataverse Web API
questions.why: You need server-side code that can connect to the external systems (e.g. the Oracle database), retrieve and TRANSFORM the data, and write it into Dataverse. An Azure Function is ideal for that ETL/transformation logic, and a WebHook triggers the function when the source changes. Microsoft Graph API accesses Microsoft 365 data, not CompanyA's line-of-business apps, so the Custom Connector/Power Automate + Graph pairs do not fit. A Web Resource is client-side content in model-driven apps and is not a data-transformation mechanism.

questions.q: CompanyA has a high number of invoices, contracts, and identity-related documents stored in an Oracle database. It wants to migrate these documents to Microsoft Dataverse and associate them with the relevant customer in the Dynamics 365 sales app. You need to save the customer's documents in Microsoft Dataverse without consuming much database storage. Which three components should you use? Each correct answer presents part of the solution.
questions.why: To store documents WITHOUT consuming Dataverse database storage, enable SharePoint document management integration: the files live in SharePoint while being surfaced against the Dataverse record. Use a Console Application (C#) to run the one-time migration from the Oracle database, and call the Dataverse Web API from that app to create the document location and associate the documents with the correct customer record. Notes (annotations) and Azure Blob Storage would either consume Dataverse storage or not integrate document management the required way, and Power Automate is not needed for the bulk migration.

questions.q: Users with Global Admin rights must be able to manage users in the organization that are using the mobile app. You need to check that the role assigned to logged-in mobile users is appropriate before allowing them to access the mobile app. In which order should you perform the actions?
options: Call Custom Connector in Canvas app.
Grant Admin consent to API permissions.
answer: Grant Admin consent to API permissions.
Call Custom Connector in Canvas app.
questions.why: To read a user's roles you call Microsoft Graph. First register an application in Microsoft Entra ID. Then request the required Microsoft Graph API permissions on that app registration. Grant admin consent so the permissions are effective for the organization. Wrap the Graph call in a Custom Connector (so it can be used with low-code). Finally call the Custom Connector from the Canvas app to check the logged-in user's role before granting access.

questions.q: You need to use components that enable customers to track their order shipment status without needing to interact with any employees. Which two components should you use? Each correct answer presents a complete solution.
questions.why: Both provide customer self-service with no employee involved. A Microsoft Copilot Studio chatbot lets a customer ask for their order status and get an automated answer. A Power Pages site (external-facing portal) lets customers sign in and view/track their orders themselves. Unified Service Desk and Omnichannel are agent-facing tools that require employees, and a Business Process Flow guides internal users through a process rather than giving customers self-service tracking.
